steps.title: Spot the parallel verb structure
steps.content: The sentence describes the temperature using three verbs across three months: “is 62.6 degrees in June,” “[___] to 66.6 degrees in July,” and “dips slightly to 62.6 degrees in August.” These three verbs must be in the same tense and form.

steps.title: Match the verb tense to complete the parallel
steps.content:
”Rises” is simple present, the same tense as “is” and “dips,” creating a grammatically parallel series.

The other options—”has risen” (present perfect), “rising” (present participle), and “rose” (past tense)—each use a different tense or form, breaking the parallel.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
